dc-record.title: School Office & Kindergarten
Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: Photo of Monte Rio School exterior during or immediately after 1986 flood. Shows moderne font sign "MONTE RIO SCH" two double hung windows below the sign. Water level is just below windows. A front door is to the left of the windows. On right is wing of school with entry awning on two metal posts over solid panel entrance door, two windows with closed louvres, one on left of awning, the other on the right. To the right of the entry awning is a floating dumpster.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: View of Russian River and Monte Rio from Starett Hill area. St. Catherine's Church is visible on hill. Large structure (dance hall?) on site of Rio Theater and many homes visible to the left of the Monte Rio bridge. Most hills have had redwood trees chopped down and mountains have been de-forested. Photo may have been taken in winter when river was running high.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: Blurry view of Monte Rio bridge from center span. Deforested hills in background. Construction under right side of bridge may be boat landing. Monte Rio Sandy Beach under left side of bridge.

dc-record.title: John Houseman Memorial Ambulance wi/ Volunteer Frieman & Red Cross Volunteers
Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: BW Photo. Dedication of the John Hausman Memorial Ambulance April 15, 1951.Ambulance with Monte Rio Fire Dist 3 on door side. Commemeration plaque top of door. Monte Rio Emergency on side panel. Log retaining wall behind ambulance. 13 men and women standing or sitting in front of ambulance. Verso: This Photo is #......... 8x10 Copies May Be Obtained By Sending $ To Redwood Photo Studio Guerneville, Calif. + Phone 15R Dedication of John Hausman Memorial Ambulance 4/15/51 Al Bohng, Gilson Willett, Lee Torr lll, Lois Fulkerson, Inez Litton, Florence Crawford. Seated: Harry Burke Scott, Dorothy Meadows, Emma Hess, Mary Balish, Marge Pedroia. On ground: Chas Rickett. Maury Darr Photo. From Etta Rickett 12/9/80

dc-record.title: Markham R.R. Trestle
Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: BW Photo. (Ed Morril). River in foreground Trestle in middle of photo. Slopes with some trees in upper background behind trestle. Verso: 4 sec no filter. On August 19, 1885 the Northwestern Railroad Co. of California was incorporated and in accordance with an agreement with the North Pacific Coast Railroad the latter was to construct a trunk line across the Russian River and up willow Creek. This picture shows the trestle crossing the Russian River just short of Markham. This trestle was operational only the summer months during low water and was washed out in 1895. Source: The Northwest Pacific Railroad, Copyright 1964 by Fred A. Stindt and Guy L. Dunscomb LCCC#64-24033. 2nd photo is from Sonoma County Historical Society Collection. View of Bridge at Markham's crossing on Russian River, Jenner, California, late 19th century. This is the bridge that was built after the earlier one washed away in 1895. We are still researching to find more information.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic

dc-record.title: Digital File
dc-record.description: Digital File: scan of color photo: Image of framed art and cursive certificate given to Clare Harris on the day of the transfer of Johnson Beach to new owners. Wording: "The Russian River Life on the river - It is an addiction It gets in your blood Love - Laughter and friends are always welcome. Life along the river. Immerse yourself in nature. slow down and meander go around the obstacles. Be thoutful of those downsteram stay current. The beauty is in the light of the pale moon. After all it is just another day in paradise where memories linger."
dc-record.subject: Digital File
Johnson's Beach
Harris, Clare

dc-record.description: Digital File: scan of sepia photo: Photo of horseless carriage in downtown Santa Rosa. Gas station and bank, both with classic doric columns in background. The car is a Hulsen automobile sold by Herbert Harris (brother of Clare Harris) of Rio Nido for $300 to a Santa Rosa auto dealer circa 1938. Photo is earlier. 1910?

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: Two black and white photographs of the Campbells taken in Monte Rio in 1916 apparently on the same day. One is of Dick and Margaret Campbell standing together, she with round floral display and bow, he with hat. The second photo is she alone wearing the same long dress tied a waist with ribbon and flower. The accompanying note gives details about the couple.

dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
North Pacific Coast Rail Road (narrow-gauge) Engine #21 at Howards Station (Occidental). Overexposed print showing North Pacific Coat Railroad Engine #21. in foreground with Howard Train Station on other side of train. Photo shows engine, a boiler car, a freight car, and a passenger car. Victorian homes in background. Engine #21 was an experimental train engine built in the Sausalito shops by NPC in 1901. It was the first water-tubed boiler, oil-burning, cab-in-front locomotive in the United States.
Sources: The Northwestern Pacific Railroad, p.g. 29. Coyright 1964 by Fred A. Stindt and Guy L. Dunscomb. Library of Congress #64-24033 Just Before Yesterday, pg. 90 by Lorna Dracke, Copyright 1982
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Engines
North Pacific Coat Railroad
Engine #21
Trains

dc-record.title: Postcard
dc-record.description: BW Photo. Water Carnival Monte Rio. Gravenstein Apple Float (1925). Sebastopol is written on float. Butterfly on front. One woman sitting in apple. Beach with flags in background. Printing on bottom: Water Carnival Monte Rio, Cal. Aug 23-25. Johnson Photo. Verso: Gravenstein Apple float_ with butterfly in front contributed by Sebastopol. Note poles and strings of lights along edge of water plus flags_ (all lit up at night.) Also large spot lights shone down from Monte Rio Bridge onto floats_ The water parade started at Little Sandy Beach and continued below Monte Rio Bridge. This copy of postcard has 1925 date on it. Ruth Sheridan Witmer From Ruth Sheridan Witmer

.Early Water Carnivals

dc-record.description: A crowded scene at Monte Rio beach at turn of 19th century. Photo show launch with fringed canopy tied up to pier next to pedestrian bridge crossing Russian River from one Monte Rio beach to antoehr. Men in suits and hats standing on the beach with several beach pavilions in background.

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
Three black and white photographs of the Campbells taken in Monte Rio in 1916 apparently on the same day. One is of Dick and Margaret Campbell standing together, she with round floral display and bow, he with hat. The second photo is she alone wearing the same long dress tied a waist with ribbon and flower. The third photo appears to be a slightly different pose of the second. The accompanying note gives details about the couple.
Three small sepia photos, two of which have copies.

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
Three black and white photographs of the Campbells taken in Monte Rio in 1916 apparently on the same day. One is of Dick and Margaret Campbell standing together, she with round floral display and bow, he with hat. The second photo is she alone wearing the same long dress tied a waist with ribbon and flower. The third photo appears to be a slightly different pose of the second. The accompanying note gives details about the couple. 1 sepia print and 1 black and white copy.
Three small sepia photos, two of which have copies.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Campbell, Margaret

dc-record.title: Fond Recollections
Scrapbook
dc-record.description: This album with 79 original photographs beloned to Frank Charles Miller and Bessie Mae Lindley-Miller. All pictures were taken in the summer of 1905, taken in Guerneville and surrounding area. Guerneville was the location of the Cole family home on Main/First Street. The Cole home in these pictures is the second on the same location. The first house was lost in the fire of 1894. A detailed list of family members and their age when these photos were taken is included in the photo album. #1: Cole/Bagley/Miller in row boat #2: Cole/Heffron/Miller in row boat, pedestrian bridge (Monte Rio?) in background #3: Boat & bathers #4: Couple at summer dam #5: Summer dam #6: Train coming over tresle bridge #7: Boater with old bridge in background #8: Bather with line of observers on walkway in front of chaning rooms #9 Ladies marching with parasols (Santa Rosa?) #10 Ladies in white marching with band behind them #11 6-horse drawn carriage with ladies in white #12 horse drawn float #13 Bessie Mae Lindley-Miller in boys clothes #14 Bessie Mae Lindley-Miller seated in man's suit #15 Bessie Mae Lindley-Miller in man's suit #16 Standing (one on shoulders of another man) in front of redwoods #17 Two men sitting on summer dam #18 Redwood tree with sign #19 Frank Miller, Lillian Leah Lindley, Willam Stewart Lindley Fred Strele standing on train cow catcher #20 Four in boat #21 Six (Frank Miller, Bessie Lindley, Wm. Stewart Lindley Fred Strale, Lillian Lindley) in long boat #22: Bessie & Frank in long boat with two square sails #23: Bessie & Frank in long boat with two square sails #24; Man midair diving into the river #25: Wm. S. Lindley, Frank Miller, Fred G. Cole in river wearing wool bathing costumes #26 Frank Charles Miller, Bessie Mae Lindley, unknow woman sitting on the beach fully clothed #27: Lillian Leah Lindley, Fred Strahle, Bessie Lindley Frank Miller: two couples standing on meadow in front of redwood trees (Northwood?) #28; Wm. Stewart Lindley, Fred Strahl, Bessie Lindley, Frank K. MIller Standing on log across creek #29: Crowd of people stading next to passenger train, building to right (Monte Rio?) #30: Wm. S. Lindley, Fred Strahle, Bessie Lindley, Frank Miller standing on log across creek. #32: Fred Strahle, in jacket and hat, standing in row boat. #33: Fred Strahle, Lillian Linddley, Sm Turner, Catherine Turner, Bessie Lindley standing in front of family home on First St., Guerneville. #34: Frank Miller, Fred Strahle in dedroom of Cole family home #35: Amy Bagley & Frank Hatch (couple with open umbrella &, Bessie Lind & Frank Miller (with closed umbrella) in front of double redwood tree. #36: Frank Miller, Besslie Lindley, Edna Cole Bagley, Amy L. Bagley, Fred Strahle, Lillian Lindley, Mary Ellen Cole-Rock, John Rock on railroad tracks #37: View of Russian River #38: View of Filfe's Creek (?) & Russian River #39: View of Russian River #40: Shed in Russian River #41: Sitting on Summer Dam #42: Men dragging canoe ast summer dam #43: Fred Strahle & Lilian Lindley & unidentified coupel sitting on redwood stump with many stumps in background (Stumptown?) #44: Fred and Lillian on railroad trestle bridge with river below. #45: Frank Hatch, Amy Bagley, Bessie Lindley, Frank Miller, Edna Cole Bagley, Lillian Lindley in long row boat on River; house on deforested hill above river. #46: Fence with boat in background on river #47 Double sailed row boat (Ethel) stailing on river with railroad tracks in foregraound. #48 Cole family house, downtown Guerneville, Fred Strahle, Lillian Lindley, Cathline Turner, Sm Turner, Frank Miller, Bessie Lindley #49 Three men in row boat with others hanging on in water #50 Standing on dock in front changing room on river #51 Edna Cole-Bagley, Amy K. Bagley, Cathline Turner, Wm. Turner, in double sailed boat named Ethel. #52: Bessie Lindley & Lillian Lindley standing on rail of summer dam #53: B&W view of Patterson Point Preserve, Villa Grande 1905 #54: B&W view of Patterson Point Preserve, Villa Grande 1905 #55: Frank Miller, Bessie Lindley, Gladys Bagley under tree #56: Bessie Lindley, Frank Miller under parasol on beach with boat named Ethel in water. #57: Frank and Bessie in bathing costumes sittind on edge of row boat. #58 Frank and Bessie in bathing costumes standing on edg of river with boat in background. #59 Frank and Bessie in bathing costumes standing on diving platform #60 Frank and Bessie in bathign costumes lying on beach #61 Bessie Lindley fully dressed standin on beach at bow of rowboat #62 Wm Turner in white hat, pants, and tie standing in rowboat named "Maude", Cathline Turner a young girl is standing with row in rowboat named "Amy". Other rowboats: "Maude" and "Kitty" #63 Frank Miller rowing boat #64 Frank Miler in hat rowing boat #65 Frank & Bessie fully dressed sitting at stern of boat #66 Frank Miller formally dress in stern of boat #67 Frank & Bessie sitting under awning #68 Frank and Bessie in meadow in front of redwood trees #69 Bessie rowing, Frank with parasol at stern #70 Frank rowing with Bessie with parasol at stern. #71 Bessie in man's clothes #72 Bessie under twig-frame structure with open parasol on ground. #73 Lillian Lindley & Frank Miller on railroad track bed #74 Fred Strahle and Bessie Lindley on railroad trestle bridge #75 Bessie standing in rowboat #23 #76 Fred Strahle standing in rowboat #77 Frank Miller standin on railroad tracks #78 Bessie Lindley standing on railroad tracks #78 Lillian Lindley in feathered hat and Bessie Lindley at Guerneville Train Station #79 Lillian Lindley in feathered hat with Fred Stahle at passenge train (Guerneville Train Station)

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
Photos of Hiram Epperly's Saloon in Guerneville in 1875. The saloon was open from 1871-1880. The photo shows the saloon on the left with a group of men in front with three men on the balcony. On the right is another single story building with a wood fence. A female, possibly a child, stands at the door. In the roadway is a horse-drawn buggy.
The paper copy of the photo has been cropped slightly differently. In it, we see a building, possibly a barn, on the left. While the building on the right is barely seen.
In 1876 Washington Finely and Epperly had an argument in which Finley stabbed Epperly. Epperly then shot Finley in the chest.
Original in Healdsburg Museum.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
J.A.P. Donning
dc-record.creator: J.A.P. Donning

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
Two men sawing a redwood tree. The saws appear to be almost five feet in length. A third saw is visible at the left. Both men are in workclothes and hats.
John Schubert's Guerneville, the Early Days.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Buildings 1800's

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
Single story building with several large windows. Five men and two boys on porch with one of the men playing a fiddle. At the side of the building is a family with husband and wife and four children...all seemingly in their Sunday best. Man is holding package.
Found in John Schubert's Guerneville, the Early Days.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Buildings of the 1800's

dc-record.title: Logging at Korbel's Mill
Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
BW Photo. 17 men sitting or standing around several felled Redwoods on logway at Korbel's Mill. Sept. 12, 1875.
Logging. Four large redwood logs with eighteen men sitting or standing around the on the logs. Several of the men appear to appear to be better dressed as they are wear jackets.
Pictures are taken a Korbel's Mills about four miles from Guerneville.
Found in John Schubert's Guerneville, the Early Days.
Original in Healdsburg Museum.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Logging
Joesph P. Downing
dc-record.creator: Joesph P. Downing

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
Pippin House in Guerneville. A rather large single-story house with a porch at the right. Five men, two women, and four children are scattered around the house. The house is fronted with small garden. At the right is another house only slightly visible.
Found in John Schubert's Guerneville, the Early Days.
Original in Healdsburg Museum.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Buildings of the 1800's
Joesph P. Downing
dc-record.creator: Joesph P. Downing

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
One story house with fence. Four women and two men in yard while another man stands with his horse at the fence. In the yard is a garden with a couple of large redwood stumps. Behind the house with large flat area going all the way to the hill in the background stands row after row of redwood stumps.
Found in John Schubert's Guerneville, the Early Days.
Original in Healdsburg Museum.
Found in John Schubert's Guerneville, the Early Days.

Guerneville, looking west down 1st Street. Businesses seem to line the north side of the street while the left seems residential. A Model T is seen on the right. Further down the street are later model cars possibly from the 30's or 40's. Found in John Schubert's Guerneville, the Early Days.

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: Photo of aftermath of flooding of 1937showing effects in Monte Rio along Dutch Bill Creek where several businesses' foundations were undermined. (These buildings were just to the left of the Pink Elephant.) Johnny's Place, an Italian restaurant, can be seen at the right where it collapsed. Several other building can bee seen. Seemingly they have collapsed onto their foundations. (Note: After the wreckage was cleared away, pilings were put in to prevent further erosion.) Groups of people surveying the damage. Four nurses also seen in picture.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Monte Rio
Flooding 1937

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: Black and white photo of 1955 flood in Monte Rio. Bartlett's is in the center of the photo with buildings on either side. All buildings have about a foot and a half of water standing in the front. Expensive car can be seen in the background with water up to the wheel-wells.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Monte Rio
Flooding 1955

dc-record.title: Monte Rio Flood, Dec. 12, 1937

dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Monte Rio
Flooding 1937

dc-record.title: The Bungalo, Monte Rio, Cal.
Photograph
dc-record.description: Black and white photo. On the left is a one-story log building, the Bungalo, with a small dormer window at the center. A fenced area with tables is at the side of the building. Another fenced area is beyond the patio which might be another patio area or possibly a bridge.On the right is a small group of trees several of which have been cut down leaving large stumps.
dc-record.subject: Photograph
Monte Rio
The Bungalo

dc-record.title: Photograph
dc-record.description: Black and white photo. Group of people at a store in Monte Rio. (Thomas and Marie Niclaes at front) The Oxford Hotel sign is off to the left possibly indicating that the storefront is part of it. Six men, two women, and three children stand or sit on stools in front of the open storefront. A closed awning, American flag, and old-fashioned cash-register are visible.
dc-record.subject: Photograph
Monte Rio
Storefronts
Groceries
Drug Store
Niclaes, Thomas
Niclaes, Marie (Northern)

dc-record.title: Photograph
dc-record.description: Black and white photo. Everybody's Place, a local sweetshop in Monte Rio. Proprietors (Diamond and Samuel Niclaes) stand in front of counter. Cigarettes, beer, soda, and tobacco containers line the shelves behind the counter. The counter has a milkshake maker and jar of straws on it while ads for National Ice Cream and Chesterfields decorate the front.

Monte Rio
Storefronts
Groceries
Drug Store
Naclaes, Samuel

dc-record.title: Photocopy
dc-record.description:
Two photos, one 8" x 10" with a smaller version framed in old goldtone frame.
The photo shows the Forestville streetcar with six men standing beside it and one at the station door. Also Russell's Forestville and Guerneville Auto-Stage with five men waiting near the streetcar. A large station is in the background along with a covered waiting area.
People in photo: Left to right. Templeman, Gillespie, Tom; Close, Frank; Silk, Tom; Orchard; Johnson, Jim

dc-record.title: Photocopy
dc-record.description:
Photo framed in old goldtone frame. The photo appears to be a companion piece to the last photo 2019.01.285 which shows the Forestville streetcar at the station. The Russell's Forestville and Guerneville Auto-Stage is seen in that picture at the top left. Here the auto-stage fills the entire frame. Two men sit in the driver's seat at the front while twelve men and boys ride in the back.
Included here is a clipping from the Way Back When article in the Russian River News of 9/19/74.
dc-record.subject: Photocopy
Forestville
Auto-Stage

dc-record.title: Photocopy
dc-record.description:
Vacation Beach's Russian River Inn. The picture appears to be possibly from the 1950's or before. This is a view taken from across the river so that the beach and "kiddy's" pool are in the center of the photo. Umbrellas and sunbathers can also be seen on the beach. Above the beach is the Russian River Inn several stories high with a large sign on the roof. Stairs accessing beach can be seen to right of the main building. Serval buildings are seen both on the right and left of the main building. Also several buildings are visible behind the inn..
See attached.article by John McCarty, Saturday, February 6, 2016.
dc-record.subject: Photocopy
Guernevwood
Inns

dc-record.title: Monte Rio--Bridge, Beach, and Town--1940's
Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: An aerial view of Monte Rio from the 1940's. The bridge can be seen at the center of the photo. At the left is a large beach. 116 is not seen in the picture. On the right of the photo is downtown Monte Rio's Main Street with Moscow Road running perpendicular to Main. Several hotels can bee seen along the river's edge.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Monte Rio

dc-record.title: The New Beach at Monte Rio Is Superb
Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: Monte Rio Beach as seen from across the river. The beach appears large and quite crowded. A concession stand and parking is also visible. Umbrellas cover the beach while swimmers can be seen in the water. A couple of boats can also be seen.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Monte Rio
Beaches

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: Rio Nido Beach in its heyday. A large beach can be seen at center of the photo. A boardwalk is seen next to the concession stand that leads to the bathrooms. A small boathouse is next to the summer bridge. (This small wooden bridge was the walkway across the river to and from the trail that lead up to 116 and Rio Nido itself.) Many beachgoers can seen both in and out of the river. Canoes and other small boats are seen in the water.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Rio Nido
Beaches

dc-record.title: Happy Moments - Cannibal Island Monte Rio 1936
Photograph
dc-record.description: B&W. Four men in pasture.. one in bathing suit holding a rifle, on on horseback with serape, third man standing on wagon holding long stick/switch? , and fourth man stanidin at rear of carriage. Carriage has words "S.F. Beach Comb.." written on it and two round life preservers are propped up against the wagon with "Str. Tom Daley Life Buoy" the other buoy says "Beach Combers" Verson statement by donor: Charlie Valencia family members. Did outdoor shows in glen (MR Amphitheater). Cannibal Isand is also Frog Island, across from the Village Inn Hotel in Monte Rio.
dc-record.subject: Photograph
Cannibal Island
Frog Island
Village Inn Hotel
San Francisco Beach Combers
Valencia Family
Rhea Photo
dc-record.creator: Rhea Photo
dc-record.relation: IsReferencedBy:see 2020.05 Photos of Oscar Ekstrom and gay men of Cannibal Island

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
Panorama of downtown Guerneville during the 1986 Flood. Four separate photos are mounted together on a foam-core matt measuring 12" x 35"
Logging. Four large redwood logs with eighteen men sitting or standing around the on the logs. Several of the men appear to appear to be better dressed as they are wear jackets.
Pictures are taken a Korbel's Mills about four miles from Guerneville.
Found in John Schubert's Guerneville, the Early Days.
Original in Healdsburg Museum.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Ron Hemmerich
dc-record.creator: Ron Hemmerich

dc-record.title: Monte Rio Prk, on the Russian River
Photograph
dc-record.description: Photo showing Terraces and West view of Russian River with Mont Rio ferry and another boat in foreground. Verso of photo (to be framed): Country Home Resorts of Monte Rio Park, Mesa Grande and Sheridan in Sonoma County, California "For two miles along the beautiful wooded banks and the sloping hillsides of the majestic Russian River, at Monte Rio, Mesa Grande and Sheridan, 1,100 sightly lots, averaging 40 x 100 feet, have been laid out , and over five miles of broad terraced roads have been built, all piped with pure mountain spring water. Seven hundred lots have been sold at an average price of $50.000, and hundreds of lovely rustic cottages nestled among the magnificen Laurel, Oak, Pine and stately Redwwod trees have been built, large and small, expensive and inexpensive, all with roomy and comfortable porches overlooking the river and surrounings. They ae ideal country home resorts that cannot be excelled for picturesqe beauty, health, comfort or pleasure. Magnificent bathing, boating, and fishing in the Russian River, and beautiful drives and walks over mountain roads and trails. By owning a country home at one of he above plaes you will avoid the large expense and discomfort encountered at public resorts, it being situated in a place of your choice, arranged to your taste and comforts. I awaits you. All the trouble and inconvenience you are put to is to unlock he door, take down the blinds, let in the sun and air, and you are settled. You live and dress as you please, eat what you care for, invite such friends as you want, and are not annoyed by people who are uncongenial. When compelled to leave, you do so with regret, and look forward longinly o the time when you can return. There being a great demand, many rent their homes before or after their outing, making it a profitable as well as enjoyable investment, thus paying for its original cost in a few seasons. A typical rustic cottage can be built of rough common lumber, a good floor and liberal allowance of rustic porch, with a roof of shingles or ready made roofing, (the latter being easily and quickly laid), and the necessary doors and windows. This will cost very little and can be bult by most any man or youth. Beautify same at your leisure by shingling the outside walls and lining the inside with inexpensive burlap. Plant a few hop-roots among the ferns and around it, and you have a beautiful comfortable cotage at a very small expense, the value of which will increse yarly. Lumber mills in the mmediate vicinity supply materials for constructio purposes at current prices, and carpenters and contractors reside in the Colonies who will gladly furnish bids and estimates to any intening builders. For Maps and Particulars apply at Office of the North Shore Land Co., Room 980, Ninth Floor, Flood Building, Market Street, San Francico, Cal. F.B. Latham, Secretary

Monte Rio Park
Latham, L.B.

This scrapbooks contains 108+ b&w photographs and postcards of Oscar Ekstrom and the Beachcombers from 1930's to 1950's. They created a special camp on "Cannibal Island" in Monte Rio, across from the Highland Dell.
